bookkeeper.dual_hat_disclosure: This current Codex session participated in stage design and now also acts as bookkeeper at the user's explicit request. It must not implement, fix, or issue a formal review verdict. It remains provider/session-independent from the planned claude_glm implementer.

breakdown_author.routing_reason: Human operator explicitly selected Opus 4.8 for this breakdown node instead of the workflow's Fable5-first default.
breakdown_author.prior_involvement: The same Anthropic session previously performed the read-only software_architect design review and then authored this development breakdown.

notes: Frontend integration is light and may remain inside the backend-dominant bounded task subject to development breakdown confirmation.
The current Codex designer/bookkeeper session is not eligible for implementation or fix authorship.

rationale: Bounded backend-dominant cache/publication redesign with one new row-snapshot contract and light frontend integration.
No trading execution, full-universe prewarm, generic scheduler, concurrency pool, disk persistence, or delta-fetch merge is in scope.

pre_review_findings.title: Endpoint timeout is incorrectly reported as ok while the queued command remains unfinished
pre_review_findings.evidence: snapshot_service.py:get_symbol_snapshot defaults cmd.refresh_status=None to ok after the bounded wait expires
pre_review_findings.required_repair: Return a local timeout projection when the command is unfinished at deadline; request threads must not mutate command/cache/publication.

pre_review_findings.title: Already-expired queued command still starts new upstream I/O
pre_review_findings.evidence: snapshot_service.py:_handle_refresh_command checks deadline only after premium/history/private reads
pre_review_findings.required_repair: Pre-I/O expiry short-circuit and a second expiry check after assembly before any domain-cache commit or publication.

pre_review_findings.title: Disabled live worker can return a misleading successful refresh status
pre_review_findings.evidence: get_symbol_snapshot returns default ok when a published state exists but _worker_running() is false
pre_review_findings.required_repair: Return last-good row with timeout and warning, with zero upstream I/O.

pre_review_findings.title: Frontend treats timeout and partial one-row responses as unqualified success
pre_review_findings.evidence: Grok pre-review session 019f577e-d69a-7cb3-9e31-26f49fc542fb confirms fetchSymbolSnapshot does not consume refresh_status/warnings
pre_review_findings.required_repair: Show a non-blocking timeout or partial warning while retaining the targeted row/drawer patch and avoiding a full-table rerender.

pre_review_findings.title: Defensive click cold path can call full-universe fetch_raw
pre_review_findings.evidence: snapshot_service.py:_handle_refresh_command fetches base_raw when absent, contrary to the no-fetch_raw click-path contract
pre_review_findings.required_repair: Return timeout plus warning with zero upstream I/O when base_raw is absent.

pre_review_findings.title: History cache can commit before selected-click schema validation fails
pre_review_findings.evidence: snapshot_service.py writes _funding_history_cache before _publish(), whose first action is jsonschema.validate()
pre_review_findings.required_repair: Validate before every selected-click domain-cache commit and publish; prove schema failure leaves state, history cache, and private-input bundle unchanged.

pre_review_findings.title: Schema validation can cross deadline before cache commit/publication
pre_review_findings.evidence: snapshot_service.py checks deadline before _validate(), but commits history cache and publishes immediately after validation returns
pre_review_findings.required_repair: Re-check deadline after successful validation and before any cache commit or _publish_validated; prove validation crossing deadline leaves state/cache/private inputs unchanged.

pre_review_repairs.residual_risk: No live signed capture was performed; public raw samples and sanitised fake-key force-TTL behavioral evidence are present. Live signed capture remains an operator-authorised follow-up, not a claimed fact.

resolved_findings: Shared monotonic command deadline now gates domain-cache commit and PublishedState replacement after request timeout.
The v1 frontend one-second guard and in-flight suppression are frozen; only backend post-completion cooldown is deferred to v1.1.
Selected-asset force-TTL names the exact single-asset E2/E2b/maxBorrowable keys and preserves scheduled multi-asset batch keys.
Offline synchronous compatibility is isolated from live kill-switch-disabled last-good/503 behavior.
development_breakdown_validation.implementation_clarification: Treat deadline equality as expired: time.monotonic() >= deadline_monotonic.

review_2.model_selection_reason: The human operator supplied the completed Opus 4.8 review session and explicitly authorized commit, push, and merge after reviewing its disclosure.
review_2.reviewer_prior_involvement_notes: Anthropic provider performed design review and the development breakdown. Current Codex session is design author and user-authorised bookkeeper, so cannot issue a formal final verdict. No other registered, provider-isolated, design-uninvolved decision model exists.

next_action: Bookkeeper commits review-2 evidence, validates pre-accept, pushes the stage branch, and merges it into main under the user's explicit authorization.
